capabilities.capability: 3. Actor discovery (which real-world actors matter, grounded in evidence)
capabilities.current_behavior: The only V2 'discovery' path is one LLM JSON call: compiler.py:31-59 (_DECOMPOSE_PROMPT) asks the LLM for an 'entities' list given evidence truncated to 4000 chars (compiler.py:113 `evidence[:4000]`); compile_world (compiler.py:103-207) passes the proposals through UNVALIDATED as raw dicts (compiler.py:196 `entities=list(raw.get("entities") or [])`) — there is zero checking that a proposed entity appears in the evidence, no alias/dedup resolution, no importance scoring, no recall check. materialize.build_world (materialize.py:31-41) then instantiates each dict as an Entity and — critically — stamps every LLM-proposed field value with `status="observed", method="compiler:evidence"` (materialize.py:37), i.e. LLM guesses are labeled as ground-truth observations, which per the Provenance contract (state.py:38-55) exempts them from particle sampling, rejuvenation (posterior.py `_rejuvenate` skips observed) and forces versioned overwrites on assimilation. In every actual benchmark run actors are HAND-LISTED, not discovered: enron.py:228-231 and 403-413 build exactly one generic entity `Entity(identity="recipient")` per message (the real person's identity never enters the world; it only enters via fitted per-recipient rates in features); behavior_games.py:258-264 hardcodes `actor`/`partner`; higgs.py builds no V2 entities at all; the nurses test (tests/test_world_model_v2.py:330-356) uses a scripted lambda emitting hand-written `nurse_0..2`. compile_world is NEVER invoked with a real LLM anywhere in the repo — its only callers are swm/facade.py:86 (which no experiment uses with architecture='world_model_v2'; grep of experiments/ shows all wmv2_*.py runners import reference-world functions directly) and tests with scripted decompositions. The legacy path swm/engine/casting.py (CastingDirector.cast, :128-153) is the closest real implementation — one LLM call over a grounded dossier brief producing named actors/segments with normalized weights and proportional variant allocation (allocate_variants :98-121) — but despite its docstring claim 'a cast of abstractions is rejected' (casting.py:8-9) there is NO rejection code: any actor string is accepted, and an unrecognized process silently defaults to 'collective_choice' (casting.py:132). It is legacy-frozen and banned from V2 imports (tests/test_world_model_v2.py:479-498). swm/entities/public_figure.py does regex-lexicon 'inference' of a single named person's traits from web snippets (hardcoded nudge constants, e.g. `0.28 + 0.12*pos - 0.16*neg` at :158).
capabilities.why_insufficient: A production general social world model must identify the causally-relevant actors from evidence for arbitrary questions: named-entity resolution against the dossier, canonical IDs, alias merging, role inference, importance/sensitivity ranking, and abstention when the actor set is unknowable. Today the actor set is either (a) whatever one un-checked LLM completion says over a 4000-char evidence window — with the resulting fabrications permanently mislabeled 'observed', poisoning the entire downstream uncertainty machinery — or (b) a hand-written list per benchmark. There is no measurement of actor precision/recall anywhere. The V2 anti-cheating framing ('actors must come from evidence') exists only in prose.
capabilities.required_replacement: An evidence-grounded actor-resolution stage between compile and materialize: (1) extract candidate actors from the full evidence corpus (not a 4k truncation) with citations per actor; (2) canonicalize/dedup (string+embedding alias resolution); (3) validate every LLM-proposed entity against the extraction — unsupported entities get status='inferred'/'assumed' with low confidence or trigger CompileAbstention, never status='observed'; (4) importance scoring feeding the fidelity planner (explicit entity vs marginalized-into-population); (5) fix materialize.py:37 so provenance status is derived from evidence support, not hardcoded 'observed'.
capabilities.empirical_test_needed: Run compile_world with a real LLM on >=50 held-out scenarios that have human-annotated ground-truth actor lists and evidence dossiers. Measure: actor precision (fraction of proposed entities literally supported by evidence text — today unmeasured, expected low with 4k truncation), actor recall vs annotations, duplicate/alias rate, and provenance integrity (assert zero LLM-proposed fields carry status='observed'). A one-line probe already demonstrates the provenance bug: build_world on any plan yields prov.status=='observed' for every LLM-guessed field.

capabilities.current_behavior: population.py implements real, runnable math: Population.allocate (:58-77, largest-remainder allocation proportional to weight x uncertainty x sensitivity with floors), sample_particles (:79-90), check_marginals (:92-104). But it is exercised ONLY by tests (tests/test_world_model_v2.py:307-319); grep confirms no rollout code path ever reads `world.populations` — populations built by materialize.build_world (materialize.py:42-49) are inert during simulation. Empirically verified: a compiled plan with a 2-segment population runs run_from_plan fine and the population contributes zero deltas; no code converts a PopulationParticle into an Entity or into any operator input. Worse, materialize.py:46-48 hardcodes EVERY 'differs_on' heterogeneity dimension to the identical placeholder distribution {mean:0.5, sd:0.2, lo:0, hi:1} with status='assumed' — verified empirically: 'support', 'income', 'turnout' all get the same dist regardless of semantics. Segment weights are bare LLM point numbers with no evidence, and normalized_weights (:52-56) silently zeroes any weight expressed as a distribution (a StateField with dist but value=None maps to 0.0), so the documented 'weight as dist' is unsupported in practice. check_marginals — the one validation hook against real survey data — is never called outside tests; PopulationSegment.sensitivity defaults to 0.5 and nothing ever sets it from data. The Upworthy reference world's 'population' (upworthy.py:58-75, population_rank) is 200 synthetic particles perturbing fitted linear weights lognormally with a hardcoded dim_sd=0.8 'labeled broad prior' — it is benchmarked, but constructs no population from any audience data. The genuinely load-bearing population machinery in the repo is LEGACY: swm/simulation/population_simulator.py (DemographicCell, PopulationSimulator with mean-field opinion + turnout coupling, cells_from_rows :134-150 building cells from real labeled rows) and swm/state/population.py (sufficient-statistic PopulationState) — both banned from V2 by the import-boundary tests.
capabilities.why_insufficient: Production population construction means: segments derived from real marginals (census/survey/platform data) with sourced weights, per-dimension heterogeneity distributions estimated from data (not one universal N(0.5,0.2)), eligibility/turnout/exposure adjustments with provenance, particles that actually act in the simulation, and marginal-validation gates that fail loudly. Currently the V2 population layer is a well-shaped data class whose contents are placeholder constants and whose particles never touch the rollout — perturbing segment weights provably cannot change any forecast. The one validated population dynamic (legacy PopulationSimulator's coupled turnout/opinion) is walled off.
capabilities.required_replacement: (1) A population-grounding stage: map 'differs_on' dimensions to estimators (dataset marginals -> DATASET method; else llm_distribution -> LLM method; else labeled PRIOR), replacing the materialize.py:47 constant; sourced weights with provenance and support for weight distributions. (2) Wire populations into execution: a PopulationDecisionOperator (or particle->Entity expansion per the fidelity plan) so segment particles generate events/votes/adoption in the shared world. (3) Mandatory check_marginals gate against held-out observed marginals before a run is reportable. (4) Port or re-derive the legacy turnout/opinion coupling as a registered V2 mechanism.
capabilities.empirical_test_needed: Two tests: (a) sensitivity test — compile+run the same question with segment weights perturbed +/-50%; today the terminal distribution is provably identical (populations unread); after the fix it must shift monotonically. (b) marginal-recovery test — build a population from a real survey (e.g. GSS cells), sample particles, and require check_marginals to pass within tolerance on held-out marginals, plus a calibration check that within-segment heterogeneity sd matches observed within-cell variance rather than 0.2 everywhere.

capabilities.capability: 5. Institutional discovery (which rule systems govern a scenario, as executable constraints)
capabilities.current_behavior: The ENFORCEMENT core is real and tested: RuleSystem.validate_action (institutions.py:64-71) is wired into every TransitionOperator.validate (transitions.py:89-97) and into AgentDecisionOperator's candidate pre-filter (transitions.py:193-199), so an institution-invalid action genuinely cannot apply (tests/test_world_model_v2.py:111-127 passes); run_vote (institutions.py:74-88) executes deterministically via InstitutionalVoteOperator (transitions.py:337-371). But Rule.check (institutions.py:24-55) implements only 5 kinds (decision_right, deadline, budget, eligibility, procedure); the docstring advertises voting/threshold/legal/capacity, and ANY unimplemented kind silently returns (True,'') — empirically verified: Rule(kind='voting').check(...) == (True, ''). DISCOVERY is absent: the compiler prompt (compiler.py:51) asks the LLM for institutions [{id, rules:[{kind, params}]}], and materialize.py:55-59 instantiates whatever comes back verbatim — no validation of `kind` against the executable set (an LLM-proposed 'quorum' rule becomes a permanent no-op validator that always passes), no grounding of params in evidence, no library of real institutional templates (cloture, quorum, veto, contract law), and no test/benchmark ever runs institution proposal with a real LLM. Param handling is crash-prone with LLM-typical output — empirically verified: a deadline rule with by_ts='2026-06-30' (the string format the compiler prompt itself teaches for events) raises TypeError('>' not supported between 'float' and 'str') at validation time mid-rollout; a budget rule without a registered quantity defaults available to 0.0 and blocks ALL spending; institutions.py:36 computes `holder = p.get('resource_holder'), p.get('resource')` into a dead variable — the actor's own entity resources are never consulted by the budget check.
capabilities.why_insufficient: For a production model, institutional discovery means recognizing from evidence which rule systems bind (a board charter, Senate procedure, platform ToS, a signed contract), compiling them into the executable rule vocabulary, and ABSTAINING when the governing rule kind is not executable. Today the hard guarantee ('an LLM cannot produce an action that violates an executable rule') is vacuously satisfiable: propose a rule kind outside the 5 implemented ones and it validates everything; propose realistic params and the rollout can crash. Nothing measures whether proposed institutions match the scenario's real ones, and no real-world institutional benchmark exists (wmv2_forecastbench_run.py:172 explicitly logs 'institutional_kernels: UNSUPPORTED').
capabilities.required_replacement: (1) A closed, machine-checkable rule-kind registry: materialize must reject or mark-experimental any kind outside the executable set (mirror the mechanism-registry pattern at compiler.py:144-160) instead of instantiating silent no-ops. (2) Typed param schemas per kind with coercion (parse_time on by_ts; resource resolution order: entity resources -> quantities -> params.available) and compile-time validation errors. (3) An institutional template library (voting thresholds incl. quorum/supermajority/weighted, stage machines, veto/approval chains) parameterized from evidence with provenance. (4) A discovery evaluation: LLM proposes institutions from real charters/bylaws; validator scores against annotated rules.
capabilities.empirical_test_needed: Fuzz + fidelity harness: (a) feed Rule.check 1000 LLM-realistic (kind, params, action) triples and count silent-passes on unknown kinds (currently 100%) and TypeErrors (deadline-string case reproduces immediately); (b) a held-out set of ~30 real governance scenarios (board votes, union ratifications, legislative cloture) with annotated binding rules — measure rule precision/recall of the compiled RuleSystem and verify that a scenario governed by a non-executable kind produces CompileAbstention rather than an always-true validator.

capabilities.capability: 6. Graph construction (typed relationship/network state shared by decision, diffusion, institutions)
capabilities.current_behavior: network.py:61-87 RelationGraph is a flat Python list of RelationEdge dataclasses with O(E) linear scans for every query (out_edges/in_edges/edge), no adjacency index, no dedup, no referential-integrity check (edges to nonexistent entities/populations are accepted). Registered-relation typing (CORE_RELATIONS :15-27, register_relation :31-38, __post_init__ KeyError :54-57) works, but construction from a compiled plan (materialize.py:50-54) catches that KeyError and silently `continue`s — empirically verified: a plan with an unregistered relation materializes to 0 edges with no warning, directly contradicting the inline comment 'dropped loudly at compile'. Plan relations carry only src/rel/dst (compiler prompt :50), so every materialized edge gets the hardcoded default strength dist {mean:0.5, sd:0.2} status='assumed' (network.py:46-48); trust/channel/delay are never populated from evidence. Consumption is nearly nil: observable_view (transitions.py:127-129,137) lists at most 12 edges as strings in the agent prompt, and RelationshipUpdateOperator (transitions.py:267-299) applies bounded strength/trust shifts — that is the entire runtime use. The declared network semantics are dead code: observers_of (network.py:85-87), edge visibility, delay_hours, and channel are consumed by NOTHING (grep across swm/ confirms); no diffusion/exposure operator propagates information along 'observes' edges. The one benchmark that actually needs a network — Higgs diffusion — BYPASSES RelationGraph entirely: reference/higgs.py:45-65 and :129-142 stream the 14M-edge follower graph into plain defaultdicts outside any WorldState, and v2_contagion_predict (:145-188) runs contagion on index arrays, not on the V2 graph or the V2 event loop. Legacy: swm/graph/diffusion.py is an explicit stub (IMPLEMENTED=False, :9); swm/state/graph.py + swm/transition/diffusion.py contain a real adjacency-dict Graph with independent-cascade/linear-threshold/Hawkes dynamics — executable but legacy-frozen and banned from V2 imports (tests/test_world_model_v2.py:479-498).
capabilities.why_insufficient: The V2 promise is 'ONE typed graph that decisions, diffusion and institutions share'. In reality the graph is a toy: no construction pipeline from real relational data (org charts, follower edges, communication logs, co-membership), all edge strengths are the same hardcoded prior, its distinctive semantics (visibility, delay, observation frontier) are unread, silent edge-dropping hides compile failures, and it cannot hold a real network at scale (list scan per query; deepcopy of the whole WorldState per particle clone makes a large embedded graph O(particles x E) memory). The single networked benchmark had to abandon it — the strongest possible evidence it is not production graph construction.
capabilities.required_replacement: (1) Indexed adjacency storage (dict src->edges, dst->edges) with shared/immutable topology across particle clones (copy-on-write or edges held outside the deepcopied state) so 10^5-10^7-edge graphs are feasible. (2) Construction adapters from real data: edge lists (Higgs-style), communication logs (Enron sender->recipient with fitted pair strengths — the fitted pair_rate already computed in enron.py:70-73 belongs on edges), and plan relations with evidence-sourced strength/trust/delay instead of the 0.5 default. (3) Hard failure (or experimental-marking) instead of silent drop at materialize.py:54, plus referential-integrity validation. (4) An ExposureDiffusionOperator that actually consumes observes-edges, delay_hours, and visibility to schedule exposure events — making higgs-class contagion run inside the shared world (this is precisely repo task #3, 'beat exposure logistic from inside shared world'). (5) Port IC/LT/Hawkes from swm/transition/diffusion.py through the V2 operator registry.
capabilities.empirical_test_needed: (a) Scale test: load the Higgs sampled-cohort subgraph into RelationGraph, run the same 24h contagion through the V2 event loop with an observes-edge diffusion operator, and match v2_contagion_predict's per-user probabilities within Monte-Carlo error at acceptable wall-time/memory (today impossible — no such operator exists and per-particle deepcopy of the graph explodes). (b) Semantics test: two worlds differing only in an edge's delay_hours/visibility must produce different exposure timing in branch logs (today provably identical since neither field is read). (c) Construction test: compile a plan whose LLM output includes one unregistered relation and assert a loud error/experimental mark instead of the current silent 0-edge graph.

result.load_bearing_notes:
CALL PATHS. The only end-to-end V2 pipeline is swm/facade.py:forecast(architecture='world_model_v2') -> compiler.compile_world -> materialize.build_world/run_from_plan -> rollout.WorldModelV2Run.run -> init_state.InitialStateModel.sample_particles -> rollout.RolloutEngine.run_branch -> contracts.OutcomeContract.project. No experiment or benchmark ever drives this with a real LLM; every wmv2_*.py runner (enron, behaviorbench, higgs, omnibehavior, upworthy, forecastbench) imports reference-world functions directly and hand-builds WorldState/Entity/EventQueue, bypassing compiler+materialize entirely. The wmv2_forecastbench 'V2' arm is not the world model at all — it is a closed-form crowd-probability rescale p=1-(1-p_crowd_cal)^(g^(1-rem)) (wmv2_forecastbench_run.py:123-135). Task #6's '>=100 held-out generality cases' will be the FIRST real-LLM exercise of the compile->materialize path; expect the failure modes below to fire immediately.

DATA FORMATS. WorldExecutionPlan.entities/populations/institutions/relations/quantities are RAW LLM dicts (compiler.py:196-198) — typing happens only at materialize time, where failures are silently swallowed (try/except KeyError: continue at materialize.py:39-40 for non-schema fields, :50-54 for relations, :62-67 for quantities). Entity schema is the closed tuple ENTITY_FIELDS (state.py:110-113) plus register_entity_extension; Entity.set raises KeyError on anything else, and materialize converts that into a silent field drop.

CRITICAL GOTCHAS (empirically verified in this audit):
1. materialize.py:37 stamps every LLM-proposed entity field status='observed', method='compiler:evidence'. Provenance semantics are executable (observed fields are never rejuvenated/perturbed: posterior.ParticlePosterior._rejuvenate, init docstring state.py:41-43), so LLM fabrications become immutable ground truth. Fix before any real-LLM run.
2. materialize.py:47: every population 'differs_on' dimension gets the identical dist {mean:0.5,sd:0.2,lo:0,hi:1}. Population objects are never read by any operator during rollout — world.populations is write-only. Population.sample_particles/check_marginals run only in tests. Population.normalized_weights zeroes weights expressed as distributions.
3. institutions.Rule.check: unknown kind -> silently (True,''); deadline with string by_ts (the format the compiler prompt teaches) -> TypeError mid-rollout; budget rule with no registered quantity -> available=0.0 blocks all spends; institutions.py:36 'holder' is a dead variable (actor's own resources never checked).
4. materialize relation drop is silent (0 edges, no log) despite the 'dropped loudly' comment. All materialized edges get default strength 0.5. observers_of/visibility/delay_hours/channel on RelationEdge are consumed by nothing in the repo.
5. OutcomeContract.project does not enforce that terminal readout values fall in the declared options: a binary contract with options ['yes','no'] happily returned {'0.5': 1.0} when the readout quantity kept its initial value (verified via run_from_plan). Distribution keys are stringified readout values.
6. Global mutable registries are process-wide: _RELATIONS (network.py:28), _ENTITY_EXTENSIONS (state.py:115), _EVENT_TYPES (events.py:19), _OPERATORS (transitions.py:16). register_event_type is called at import/predict time by reference worlds (e.g. enron.py:237 inside v2_predict), so cross-scenario contamination between compiled worlds in one process is possible; tests rely on this global state.
7. WorldState.clone = copy.deepcopy of everything (state.py:208-213), and InitialStateModel.sample_particle deep-copies the base world per particle (init_state.py:110). Any large embedded graph/population multiplies memory by n_particles. This is why higgs.py holds its network outside WorldState.
8. Event ordering: Event is @dataclass(order=True) keyed on (ts, seq); Event.__post_init__ raises KeyError for unregistered etypes — compiler-proposed event types are auto-registered with validated=False (compiler.py:177-181) but hazard etypes are NOT registered by the compiler path (materialize queue_builder schedules them; StochasticHazard events go through Event() and will raise for unregistered hazard etypes — compiler.py:188 defaults etype to 'distraction' which is pre-registered, masking this).
9. Anti-regression tests to respect when replacing code: tests/test_world_model_v2.py enforces (a) no scenario-branch regexes in v2 source (:383-392), (b) AST import bans — v2 may not import swm.transition/swm.simulation/swm.worlds/swm.variables/legacy engine (:479-498), (c) banned tokens 'calibrated_readout|bayes_logistic|from swm.transition|from swm.variables' anywhere in v2 files (:217-224). So porting legacy PopulationSimulator/diffusion code means re-implementing inside swm/world_model_v2/, not importing.
10. Legacy assets worth mining (frozen, not importable into v2): swm/engine/casting.py (actor casting + proportional variant allocation — same largest-remainder scheme as Population.allocate, duplicated logic), swm/simulation/population_simulator.py (cells_from_rows + turnout/opinion coupling), swm/transition/diffusion.py (IC/LT/Hawkes over swm/state/graph.py Graph), swm/entities/persona.py (conjugate persona posteriors). swm/graph/diffusion.py is an empty stub (IMPLEMENTED=False) — do not mistake it for the real legacy diffusion at swm/transition/diffusion.py.
11. Enron reference world quirks: entity identity is the literal string 'recipient' (per-message single-entity worlds); reply_delay_days is stored as a fake anonymous quantity `type('Q',(),{...})()` (enron.py:273-275, 517-518), not a quantities.Quantity — any code assuming Quantity's interface on world.quantities will break on those branches. The fitted sender->recipient pair rates (build_examples feats) are the natural source for real edge strengths if the network layer is made real.
12. The V2 test suite (29 tests) passes in 0.26s — all offline, all scripted-LLM or llm=None; treat 'passing' as architecture-shape validation only, not capability validation.

capabilities.capability: 1. Question parsing (NL question -> typed outcome contract)
capabilities.current_behavior: compile_world() (compiler.py:103) formats one prompt (_DECOMPOSE_PROMPT, compiler.py:31) asking the LLM for a full decomposition JSON including {outcome: {family, options, resolution_rule, readout_var}}, parses it with swm.engine.grounding.parse_json, and builds an OutcomeContract (contracts.py:22) whose validate() checks only: family in the 11-string FAMILIES tuple, >=2 options for categorical, and readout is callable. Missing readout_var or unparseable JSON raises CompileAbstention. VERIFIED EMPIRICALLY: (a) this path has NEVER executed against a real LLM anywhere in the repo — the only callers are tests/test_world_model_v2.py:354 and :374 which pass `llm=lambda p: json.dumps(<hand-written decomposition>)`, and swm/facade.py:86; no experiment imports it; (b) facade.forecast(question, architecture='world_model_v2') with the default llm=None crashes with TypeError('NoneType' object is not callable), not CompileAbstention; (c) a garbage as_of ('whenever') raises raw ValueError from parse_time at compiler.py:194 (outside the try/except that guards horizon at :140), not an abstention; (d) horizon BEFORE as_of is accepted silently (horizon_ts < as_of, no check); (e) readout_var is never validated to exist in the proposed entities/quantities — 'nonexistent_entity.some_field' compiles and runs to {'None': 1.0}; (f) resolution_rule is stored as truncated free text and never executed or checked against anything; options are never checked against what the readout can return. The question also does not yield as_of/horizon — the caller must supply both strings out of band. Legacy: swm/api/compiler.py StructuralCompiler.compile (line 387) + build_compile_prompt (line 325) is a separate NL->ModelSpec parser for 6 legacy mechanisms; tests/test_compiler.py exercises it ONLY via cached_compile_fn replay dicts (never a real LLM in tests); anthropic_compile_fn (line 365) is the untested production backend.
capabilities.why_insufficient: The single most important production interface — real natural-language question in, valid typed contract out — has zero empirical evidence: 100% of executions use scripted decompositions written by the test author, so parse rate, abstention rate, family-assignment accuracy, and readout_var validity on real LLM output are all unknown. The validator accepts semantically broken contracts (readout pointing nowhere, horizon before as_of, options disjoint from readout codomain), so even a perfect LLM cannot be distinguished from a hallucinating one at compile time. The facade's default arguments crash instead of abstaining, so the advertised 'one public door' does not function without undocumented setup.
capabilities.required_replacement: A real question-parsing stage validated against a held-out NL question set: (1) split question parsing (family, options, horizon/as_of extraction, resolution semantics) from world decomposition so each is independently checkable; (2) compile-time cross-checks: readout_var must resolve against the proposed entities/quantities, horizon > as_of, declared options must be reachable from the readout's codomain (or an explicit value->option mapping must be emitted); (3) all input errors (bad timestamps, llm=None) must surface as CompileAbstention with reason, never raw TypeError/ValueError; (4) an LLM-in-the-loop acceptance suite (>=100 held-out questions across >=16 domains) measuring valid-contract rate, abstention precision, and family accuracy against human labels.
capabilities.empirical_test_needed: Run compile_world with a real LLM over >=100 held-out NL questions spanning domains never named in prompts/tests; report: JSON-parse rate, CompileAbstention rate with reason histogram, fraction of plans whose readout_var resolves in the materialized world, fraction where terminal distribution support is a subset of declared options, and crash rate (any non-CompileAbstention exception is a failure). Fuzz as_of/horizon (garbage strings, horizon<as_of, horizon==as_of) and assert abstention not crash.

capabilities.capability: 2. Causal-world construction (what the compiler actually builds; scripted/hardcoded per scenario?)
capabilities.current_behavior: The core is genuinely scenario-agnostic: no `if election/email/viral` branches exist in swm/world_model_v2/ (pinned by test_no_scenario_branches_in_v2_source, test_world_model_v2.py:383, and the AST import-boundary test at :479). compile_world validates mechanisms by registry membership, coerces latents to Gaussian records (mean=(lo+hi)/2, sd=(hi-lo)/4, confidence hardcoded 0.3, compiler.py:164-172), registers unknown event types on the fly with validated=False (:179-181), and computes a fidelity plan. materialize.build_world (materialize.py:26) turns plan dicts into WorldState. VERIFIED DEFECTS: (a) every LLM-proposed entity field value is stamped Provenance status='observed', method='compiler:evidence' (materialize.py:37) regardless of evidence — since 'observed' fields are exempt from particle perturbation and posterior rejuvenation, LLM guesses become unfalsifiable facts; (b) non-schema fields and unregistered relations are silently `continue`-dropped at materialize time (:40, :54) with no record in the plan; (c) population segments accept negative/unnormalized weights (verified: weights 5.0 and -3.0 accepted) and ALL heterogeneity dims get the identical hardcoded prior {mean:0.5, sd:0.2, lo:0, hi:1} (materialize.py:47); (d) world.populations is built but NO operator in transitions.py reads it — populations are inert in the plan->run path; (e) scheduled events dated before as_of compile fine and are silently skipped by rollout.py:40 ('stale event behind the clock'), producing a zero-event world; (f) the fidelity plan's explicit/marginalized lists and agent_samples are consumed by NOTHING (grep-verified: only compute_plan.n_particles is read, materialize.py:107) and its sensitivity keys are unvalidated free strings ('totally_bogus_component' accepted); _fidelity_plan is also computed twice (compiler.py:202,204). CRUCIALLY, every real benchmark bypasses this pipeline entirely: all 8 experiments/wmv2_*.py import zero compiler/materialize symbols; reference/enron.py v2_predict (line 203) and behavior_games.py hand-construct WorldState + bespoke inline operators (ReplyDecision, TypedMessageDecision) per dataset; reference/higgs.py, upworthy.py, omnibehavior.py never touch the V2 runtime at all (fitted logistics/KDE/formulas); and experiments/wmv2_forecastbench_run.py's 'V2' arm is literally the closed-form p = 1-(1-platt(crowd))^(g^(1-rem)) with g grid-searched over 6 values (lines 123-135) — no world model of any kind.
capabilities.why_insufficient: The 'one compiler for every scenario' claim is validated by exactly one unit test with a hand-written decomposition (the nurses case). Everything that has produced a benchmark number was hand-built per scenario — which is precisely the pattern the architecture bans, just relocated from the engine into reference worlds and experiment scripts. The materializer fabricates 'observed' provenance from LLM guesses, silently drops mis-typed content instead of abstaining, builds population state that cannot causally affect any outcome, and computes a fidelity plan that controls nothing. A general production world model cannot be claimed while the compiled path has never generated a validated forecast and the validated paths were never compiled.
capabilities.required_replacement: (1) Provenance honesty: compiler-proposed values must enter as status='inferred'/'assumed' with the LLM prompt hash as method; 'observed' must require an evidence reference. (2) Loud rejection: dropped fields/relations/latents must be recorded in the plan (or trigger abstention if sensitivity-weighted), not silently discarded. (3) Population mechanisms: segment-level operators (or explicit marginalization) so world.populations affects the readout; weight normalization/validation; heterogeneity priors from data or declared latents, not one hardcoded Gaussian. (4) Fidelity plan must be enforced (explicit vs marginalized representation, agent_samples) or deleted. (5) Migrate at least one reference world (Enron is closest) to compile from a decomposition through materialize.run_from_plan and reproduce its benchmark numbers, proving the compiled path can carry a real evaluation.
capabilities.empirical_test_needed: (a) Re-run the Enron benchmark (experiments/wmv2_enron_run.py arms I4-I8) through compile_world+run_from_plan instead of reference/enron.v2_predict and show Brier parity; any gap quantifies how much of the reported performance is hand-built. capabilities.current_behavior: V2 'selection' is: the LLM sees a dict of {mech_id: causal_role} for the 9 registered mechanisms (compiler.py:113) and returns ids; the compiler's entire acceptance logic is `if mid in registry` (compiler.py:147) — unknown ids are rejected, free-text missing_mechanisms become never-executed experimental candidates, and zero accepted mechanisms triggers abstention. No scoring, no ranking, no domain matching, no calibration-status gating, no parameter packs. VERIFIED EMPIRICALLY: 3 of the 9 registry entries (poisson_arrival, poll_error_aggregation, whipcount_binomial — the 'v1 validated numerical kernels', mechanisms.py:72-80) have operator='' (the dataclass default); operators_from_plan (materialize.py:88-97) silently skips any mechanism with an empty operator name, so a plan whose only accepted mechanisms are these three compiles as '3 accepted mechanisms', materializes ZERO operators, runs a zero-delta rollout, and returns the initial quantity value as {'0.4': 1.0} tagged readout='terminal_states' — a fabricated-certainty answer wearing the anti-cheating provenance label. The compiler also copies m.operator into the accepted record (:151) so the plan itself documents the empty operator without anyone checking it. The new swm/world_model_v2/registry/ subpackage (record.py MechanismRecord with lifecycle/promotion_blockers, store.py RegistryStore with JSON persistence and _mirror_lean into the compiler vocabulary) is UNTRACKED work-in-progress (git status '??', mtime minutes old): data/ and families/ are empty, no __init__.py, nothing imports store.py, no tests. Legacy: swm/api/selecting_compiler.py actually implements candidate generation + validity scoring + critic ranking (tested in tests/test_selecting_compiler.py with cached specs and a stub critic), but only over the 6 legacy structural-model mechanisms.
capabilities.why_insufficient: Registry membership is not selection: with 9 mechanisms and only 6 executable, the LLM cannot be wrong in any way the compiler detects, and can be 'right' in a way that silently executes nothing. There is no notion of which mechanism fits which scenario (domains=('*',) everywhere), no competition between structural hypotheses, no calibration-status-based gating (an 'uncalibrated' mechanism is treated identically to a 'calibrated' one), and no parameter binding — accepted mechanisms carry no parameters at all. The only genuinely selective machinery in the repo (SelectingCompiler's validity+critic scoring) lives on the frozen legacy path and covers a disjoint mechanism library.
capabilities.required_replacement: A mechanism registry where every non-experimental entry MUST name an executable operator (registration-time check: get_operator(entry.operator) resolves), plus compile-time selection that (1) refuses or flags accepted mechanisms that materialize no operator, (2) scores applicability from declared domains/required_state against the proposed plan, (3) binds a parameter pack (with source labels) at acceptance, and (4) records rejected-but-plausible alternatives as structural model uncertainty. Finish and wire the registry/store.py lifecycle (promotion_blockers already encodes the right gates) so compile_world's vocabulary comes from records that are provably executable and validated, then port SelectingCompiler-style candidate ranking onto V2 plans.
capabilities.empirical_test_needed: (1) Pinning test: for every entry in known_mechanisms() with experimental=False, assert entry.operator resolves via get_operator and that a minimal plan accepting it produces >=1 StateDelta of that operator — this currently fails for 3 of 9 entries. (2) Adversarial selection eval: present decompositions where the correct mechanism is knowable (synthetic worlds with known generative process) and measure whether LLM-proposed + compiler-accepted mechanism sets recover it above chance vs. accepting everything. (3) Regression: a plan whose accepted mechanisms materialize zero operators must abstain, not emit a p=1.0 distribution.

capabilities.current_behavior: MechanismEntry declares required_state and domains (mechanisms.py:21,25) and register_operator declares requires/modifies/invariants (transitions.py:19-26), but grep confirms NOTHING ever reads them: compile_world checks only registry membership; operators_from_plan checks only the operator name; no code compares required_state against the plan's entities/institutions/quantities/information. VERIFIED EMPIRICALLY: a plan accepting institutional_vote with zero institutions, belief_update with zero information items, and resource_update with zero resources compiles without complaint and runs to a silent no-op {'None': 1.0}. What DOES execute at runtime: (a) each operator's applicable(world, event) — but that is an event-type string dispatch (e.g. `event.etype == 'collective_vote'`, transitions.py:342), not applicability validation; if the plan schedules no matching event the mechanism just never fires, indistinguishable from a correct simulation; (b) TransitionOperator.validate (transitions.py:89) institutional rule-checking of actions, which is real and tested (test_institutional_rules_reject_invalid_action) but validates actions, not mechanism fit. A parallel, genuinely executable applicability module exists — uncertainty.py's MechanismProposal.validate_typing, estimate_parameter (rejects unsourced parameters), validate_mechanism (executable() gate), detect_conflicts (double-write/circular detection), all tested at tests/test_world_model_v2.py:236-269 — but it is an ORPHAN: compile_world imports none of it, so no compiled plan ever passes through parameter rejection or conflict detection. The new registry/record.py ApplicabilityRule (domains/excluded_domains/requires_state/requires_data/time_scales/transport_risk, line 117) is richer but untracked WIP with empty data and zero callers.
capabilities.why_insufficient: Applicability is the difference between a world model and a prompt: without enforcement of required_state, the compiler will happily 'run' any scenario with any mechanism set, and the failure mode is not an error but a confident empty answer ({'None': 1.0}, readout='terminal_states'). The declared metadata creates the appearance of a checked system while every check is either dead data (required_state, domains, invariants, modifies) or a dispatch predicate. The one implemented checker (uncertainty.py) being disconnected from the compile path means its guarantees ('Monte Carlo cannot run an unsupported guess') hold only inside its own unit tests.
capabilities.required_replacement: Wire applicability into compile_world: for each proposed mechanism, (1) structurally check required_state against the plan (institutions present for institutional_vote, information items + belief targets for belief_update, etc.) and against the scheduled/hazard event types that can trigger its operator — a mechanism with no possible trigger in-window is rejected with reason or downgrades the plan to abstention if high-sensitivity; (2) run uncertainty.validate_mechanism + detect_conflicts over the accepted set inside compile_world so parameter rejection and double-write detection actually gate execution; (3) adopt registry/record.py's ApplicabilityRule as the scoring input (domain tags, requires_data, transport_risk) once the registry has real data.
capabilities.empirical_test_needed: A negative-compile suite: for each registered mechanism, construct a decomposition proposing it while omitting its required state and assert CompileAbstention (or an explicit rejected_mechanisms entry with reason 'required state absent'), plus assert that a compiled plan's accepted mechanisms each have >=1 reachable trigger event type in the queue plan. Then a run-level check: across N random valid plans, fraction of accepted mechanisms that produce >=1 StateDelta; any mechanism systematically at 0 indicates dead applicability. Today both suites would fail immediately (verified by direct probe).

capabilities.current_behavior: The projection machinery is real and exercised: OutcomeContract.project (contracts.py:41-64) aggregates weighted terminal branches — str(value) frequency tables for discrete families, weighted p10/p50/p90 + mean for continuous — and WorldModelV2Run.run (rollout.py:79-90) refuses to run without a callable readout (validate() raises ContractError; tested at test_contract_refuses_to_run_without_readout) and tags results readout='terminal_states'. The latent-ensemble test (test_hidden_state_ensemble_and_native_terminal_readout) genuinely verifies that a 50/50 latent drives the terminal distribution. make_readout (compiler.py:125-135) resolves 'quantity_name' or 'entity.field[key]' paths at read time. VERIFIED DEFECTS: (a) the readout binding is never validated — a readout_var naming a nonexistent entity/quantity compiles, runs, and returns {'None': 1.0} with the terminal_states provenance tag (false confidence, no abstention); (b) discrete distributions are keyed by str(v) with no mapping to the contract's declared options — a binary contract with options ['yes','no'] whose readout reads a bool quantity emits keys 'True'/'False' (and the flagship generality test itself asserts `set(dist) <= {'True','False','None'}`, i.e. tolerates both the mismatch and the None outcome, test_world_model_v2.py:367); (c) None is silently counted as an outcome category rather than triggering abstention or a coverage warning, so a world where nothing happened is indistinguishable from a confident 'None' forecast; (d) run_branch's max_events=500 cap (rollout.py:29) silently truncates dense worlds and still marks the branch terminal=True — the readout then reads a mid-flight state as 'terminal' with no flag; (e) the only benchmark-validated readouts bypass the contract: reference/enron.py computes its per-bucket p_by by hand-iterating branches and reading an ad-hoc `type('Q',(),{...})()` object stuffed into world.quantities (enron.py:273-274, 302), sidestepping the Quantity type registry and using contract.project only for the single p14 number; (f) UtilityFunction.score / evaluate_interventions (contracts.py:98, rollout.py:93) are executable and tested but only on the toy _mini_run world.
capabilities.why_insufficient: The constitution's core promise — 'the answer is READ from terminal states' — is only as strong as the binding between contract and world, and that binding is unchecked: the system cannot tell a simulated answer from a dangling pointer. Option/space mismatches mean downstream consumers cannot rely on the distribution keys matching the contract they asked for; None-as-outcome converts silent no-op worlds (which capabilities 11/12 make easy to produce) into confident distributions; and the one benchmark that actually validated readouts did so through a side channel that skips the typed machinery, so contract.project has never been validated against ground truth.
capabilities.required_replacement: (1) Compile-time binding check: readout_var must resolve against the materialized base world (entity+field exists or quantity registered) or the compiler abstains. (2) A value->option mapping in the contract for discrete families, validated so terminal values outside the option space (including None) are counted as 'unresolved' and reported as coverage; abstain (or widen) when unresolved share exceeds a threshold instead of normalizing over garbage keys. (3) Truncated branches (max_events hit, or queue exhausted before horizon with pending hazards) must be flagged non-terminal and either excluded with a logged count or force abstention. (4) Native multi-horizon readout (the delay-curve p_by that Enron needed) as a contract family so reference worlds stop hand-rolling it, and Quantity objects only (kill the type('Q',...) bypass).
capabilities.empirical_test_needed: (1) Ground-truth recovery test: build synthetic worlds with a known generative process, verify contract.project's distribution matches analytic frequencies within Monte-Carlo error for every family (currently only response_occurrence and continuous are meaningfully exercised). (2) Adversarial binding test: 100 compiled plans with mutated readout_vars — assert 100% are caught at compile, 0% return a distribution. (3) Coverage test: plans engineered so k% of branches never write the readout field — assert reported unresolved mass == k% and abstention above threshold, instead of today's {'None': k}. (4) Re-run the Enron bucket readout through a contract-native delay family and confirm identical Brier to the hand-rolled p_by.

result.load_bearing_notes:
CALL PATHS. The only end-to-end compiled path is swm/facade.py:forecast(architecture='world_model_v2') -> swm/world_model_v2/compiler.py:compile_world -> materialize.py:run_from_plan -> {build_world, queue_builder_from_plan, operators_from_plan} -> rollout.py:WorldModelV2Run.run -> contracts.py:OutcomeContract.project. It is invoked ONLY by tests with scripted llm lambdas; every benchmark number in experiments/wmv2_*.py comes from hand-built worlds (reference/enron.py, reference/behavior_games.py use WorldModelV2Run directly with inline bespoke operators; reference/higgs.py, upworthy.py, omnibehavior.py and the 'V2' arm of wmv2_forecastbench_run.py never touch the V2 runtime at all — the forecastbench 'V2' is p=1-(1-platt(crowd))^(g^(1-rem)) with g grid-searched, lines 123-135). Do not read wmv2_* benchmark results as evidence about the compiler.

DATA FORMATS. The LLM decomposition schema is defined only inside _DECOMPOSE_PROMPT (compiler.py:31-59). WorldExecutionPlan.entities/populations/institutions/relations/quantities remain RAW dicts until materialize; only latents are typed (LatentVariableRecord) at compile. JSON extraction uses swm.engine.grounding.parse_json — a legacy import inside function bodies that deliberately passes the AST import-boundary test (banned_prefixes at tests/test_world_model_v2.py:483 do not include swm.engine.grounding). WorldModelV2Run.run returns a TUPLE (result_dict, branches) despite the '-> dict' annotation.

GLOBAL MUTABLE REGISTRIES (process-wide, import-time populated, no reset): mechanisms._REGISTRY, transitions._OPERATORS, events._EVENT_TYPES, quantities._QUANTITY_TYPES, state._ENTITY_EXTENSIONS, network._RELATIONS. compile_world MUTATES the event-type registry as a side effect (registers proposed etypes with validated=False, compiler.py:180); Event.__post_init__ raises KeyError for unregistered etypes, so operators/queues constructed outside the compiler must register event types first (reference worlds call register_event_type inline). Test pollution across cases is possible. actor_cognition.py registers 4 more mechanism entries at import (typed_action_decision etc.), so known_mechanisms() content depends on import order.

GOTCHAS FOUND BY EXECUTION (all reproduced, none currently caught by tests): (1) MechanismEntry.operator defaults to '' and operators_from_plan silently skips empty operator names — poisson_arrival/poll_error_aggregation/whipcount_binomial are accepted by the compiler but NEVER execute; a plan with only these runs 0 events and returns the initial value at p=1.0 tagged readout='terminal_states'. (2) facade forecast with llm=None crashes TypeError; bad as_of crashes ValueError at compiler.py:194 (only horizon parsing at :140 is abstention-guarded); horizon<as_of accepted. (3) build_world stamps all LLM-proposed entity fields status='observed', method='compiler:evidence' — 'observed' is load-bearing elsewhere (posterior rejuvenation and particle sampling skip observed fields), so this fabricates unfalsifiable state. (4) Latents on a nonexistent entity are silently dropped by InitialStateModel._set_path; latents on a non-schema FIELD crash with KeyError at particle-sampling time (post-compile) — abstention does not cover materialization/run. (5) Population heterogeneity is always {mean:.5, sd:.2}; weights unvalidated; world.populations never read by any operator. (6) fidelity_plan.explicit/marginalized/agent_samples consumed by nothing; sensitivity keys are unvalidated free strings; _fidelity_plan computed twice (compiler.py:202,204). (7) queue_builder_from_plan derives its hazard RNG solely from branch_id digits, so hazard arrival times are identical across different run seeds. (8) rollout run_branch max_events=500 silently truncates and still sets terminal=True. (9) enron.py writes world.quantities['reply_delay_days'] as type('Q',(),{...})() — bypasses the Quantity registry; readout code that expects Quantity semantics will not notice.

capabilities.current_behavior: Three disconnected fragments. (a) swm/inference/filter.py is an 11-line stub: a docstring plus `IMPLEMENTED = False` — zero classes or functions; nothing imports it anywhere in the repo. (b) The V2 path does PRIOR SAMPLING, not inference: InitialStateModel.sample_particle (init_state.py:106-139) draws latents from declared distributions with pairwise CorrelationRule shifts and CoherenceRule repair, then stamps them into per-particle worlds. The only production sources of those distributions are actor_cognition.hidden_state_latents (actor_cognition.py:220-244; three hand-written Gaussians for attention/responsiveness/obligation_sensitivity) and compiler.compile_world (compiler.py:162-172), which converts EVERY LLM-proposed latent into the identical hardcoded shape: mean=(lo+hi)/2, sd=(hi-lo)/4, method='prior', confidence=0.3 — evidence never conditions the latent. llm_distribution (init_state.py:38-61), the one function that could put evidence-conditioned multimodal candidates into a latent, is called only from tests (tests/test_world_model_v2.py:272-278), never from the compiler or materializer. (c) Legacy V1 has real executable point/Gaussian estimators (swm/variables/grounded_inference.py ensemble+calibration+shrinkage, swm/variables/deep_inference.py, swm/state/latent.py HierarchicalPosterior partial pooling) with passing tests, but the AST import-boundary test (tests/test_world_model_v2.py:479-498) bans V2 from importing them. Empirically demonstrated bug in the only production correlated-latent use: CorrelationRule.adjust (init_state.py:72-77) defaults lo=0,hi=1 and sample_particle never passes the source latent's real range, so responsiveness~N(1.0,·) on [0.5,1.8] shifts attention by strength×(src−0.5) instead of (src−1.15) — measured: declared attention prior mean 0.60 (workload 0.5), actual sampled mean 0.736 (+0.14 systematic bias in the Enron level>=3 arm). Also InitialStateModel._set_path (init_state.py:95-104) writes only entity paths and silently returns when the entity is missing — a latent targeting a quantity (e.g. 'ratified') is sampled into uncertainty_meta but never written into the world.
capabilities.why_insufficient: There is no inference: no code path anywhere maps observations/evidence about an entity to a posterior over its hidden state. Every latent in every real run (enron v2_predict, behavior_games v2_game_world, materialize.run_from_plan, all experiments/wmv2_*.py) is a fixed hand-written or midpoint-formula prior. Hierarchical pooling exists only in banned legacy code. The one production joint-sampling mechanism has a demonstrated scale bug that silently biases the prior it claims to implement, and quantity-path latents vanish silently. 'Hidden-state inference' for a general social world model must condition on person-specific evidence (past behavior, dossier, network position) — currently zero bits of evidence flow into any latent distribution.
capabilities.required_replacement: A real evidence→posterior layer: (1) per-latent conditioning on grounded evidence (fitted hierarchical shrinkage person←segment←population, porting/reimplementing swm/state/latent.py inside V2); (2) compiler wiring so latents carry evidence-conditioned distributions (call llm_distribution or a fitted estimator instead of the midpoint formula); (3) joint sampling via an actual joint distribution (Gaussian copula or explicit covariance) with ranges passed correctly, replacing the pairwise mid-shift hack; (4) support for quantity-path latents in _set_path or a hard error; (5) either implement swm/inference/filter.py's amortized filter or delete the stub.
capabilities.empirical_test_needed: Latent-recovery test: build synthetic worlds with known ground-truth hidden states, emit evidence through the observation models, run the inference layer, and verify (a) posterior means correlate with truth substantially above the prior baseline, (b) declared credible intervals achieve nominal coverage, (c) joint samples reproduce the declared correlation matrix within tolerance (the current code fails this today: declared mean 0.60 vs realized 0.736). Then a real-data test: infer recipient attention/responsiveness from Enron history prefixes and show it beats the fixed prior on held-out reply prediction (Brier).

capabilities.current_behavior: No multiple-world-hypothesis machinery exists at runtime. uncertainty.py is an executable but ORPHANED pipeline: MechanismProposal/estimate_parameter/validate_mechanism/detect_conflicts/uncertainty_report are called exclusively from tests (tests/test_world_model_v2.py:236-269); grep confirms zero non-test callers. The production compiler (compiler.py:144-160) uses its own separate, much simpler accept/reject: mechanism id in registry → accepted with the registry's stored metadata; unknown → rejected/experimental. Nothing runs the 8-level parameter-source hierarchy in a real compile. posterior.py's module docstring (lines 8-10) claims 'structural-model particles ride the same mechanism (a latent whose path names a parameter/mechanism choice)' — nothing implements this: WorldModelV2Run (rollout.py:71-90) builds ONE RolloutEngine with ONE operator list shared by all particles; no operator reads a mechanism-choice latent; no per-particle structure exists. The closest real thing is offline model selection: registry/ingestion.py compare_forms (lines 113-123) scores candidate functional forms on a validation split and reports all with a 'winner' — used by registry/families/diffusion.py — but the winner is then used alone; no posterior mixture over forms enters any forecast. posterior_predictive_check (posterior.py:147-155) returns a 'suspect_model_family' flag but is a toy: threshold hardcoded 1e-3; for BernoulliDetection (no likelihood_pure) the reliability floor keeps density >= (1-reliability)*0.5 ≈ 0.05 so the flag can NEVER fire on binary observations (probe P5); and it crashes with TypeError when any particle lacks the latent path (probe P1).
capabilities.why_insufficient: The uncertainty typology (UNCERTAINTY_TYPES includes 'structural_model_uncertainty') and report schema exist, but a production system must actually CARRY competing structural hypotheses — competing mechanisms, functional forms, parameter regimes — as weighted components of the forecast, update those weights against evidence, and report model-vs-world uncertainty separately from a live decomposition. Today structural uncertainty is at most a string in a report dict produced by test-only code; every real rollout commits to a single mechanism set chosen deterministically at compile time, so forecasts are systematically overconfident whenever the mechanism itself is in doubt.
capabilities.required_replacement: (1) Per-particle mechanism assignment: extend InitialStateModel or WorldModelV2Run so particles can carry different operator sets / parameter packs, with initial weights from registry validation scores; (2) wire uncertainty.py's estimate_parameter/validate_mechanism into compile_world so the hierarchy actually gates real plans, or delete the parallel pipeline; (3) Bayesian model averaging at readout: contract.project already consumes WorldBranch.weight — populate it from structure-conditional evidence; (4) fix PPC (add likelihood_pure to all models, guard None latents, calibrate the threshold) so model-family misfit is a measurable signal, not a dead flag.
capabilities.empirical_test_needed: Model-recovery test: simulate data from mechanism A (e.g. complex contagion), run a forecast carrying particles for both A and B (simple contagion), assimilate the simulated observations, and verify posterior structural weight concentrates on A (>0.9) while the mixed forecast's held-out Brier beats the wrong-single-model forecast. Plus a misfit-detection test: feed observations generated by a mechanism outside the hypothesis set and verify the PPC flag fires at a calibrated rate (today it provably cannot for Bernoulli observations and crashes on sparse worlds).

capabilities.current_behavior: observation.py (133 lines) defines the Observation dataclass and exactly two concrete models: GaussianMeasurement (lines 56-88: sd/bias/p_missing/delay_days; likelihood_pure Gaussian density plus a reliability-flattened likelihood) and BernoulliDetection (lines 91-116: p_detect/p_false; NO likelihood_pure). Both generate() and likelihood() genuinely execute and pass one synthetic unit test each (tests/test_world_model_v2.py:396-413: missingness rate, bias, delay arithmetic, likelihood ordering). But the layer is used NOWHERE in production: grep shows register_observation_model is called only in tests and defined in observation.py; the compiler never attaches observation models to latents; the materializer builds none; RolloutEngine never generates an observation from latent state during a rollout; no experiments/wmv2_*.py file references Observation at all. Dead fields confirmed by probe: `misleading` changes nothing (likelihood identical to an honest observation with equal reliability, probe P7); `visibility` ('public | actor:<id>') is read by no code anywhere; `calibration_status` is never consulted; `reported_at` delay is generated but never enforced — assimilation happily consumes observations before they would be visible (probe P2). The registry `_OBS_MODELS` (lines 119-133) is a process-global mutable dict keyed by exact path string with silent overwrite (probe P9): two scenarios sharing a path like 'recipient.attention' clobber each other's noise models for the process lifetime. All default parameters (sd=0.05, reliability=0.8/0.85/0.9, p_detect=0.95, p_false=0.02) are invented constants with calibration_status='prior' and no fitting path.
capabilities.why_insufficient: A general social world model needs a library of CALIBRATED measurement processes (polls with fitted house effects, platform analytics with measured undercount, media reporting with selection bias, counts/rates, ordinal surveys, text reports) attached per-scenario by the compiler and exercised during rollout so simulated worlds emit the observables that real evaluation data consists of. What exists is two toy families with made-up constants, a global-singleton registry unsafe for concurrent scenarios, four dead fields (misleading, visibility, calibration_status, delay-enforcement), no categorical/count/text observation type, no fitting/calibration machinery, and — decisively — zero integration: the forward direction (generate) is never invoked by the simulator, and the world already has a SECOND, unconnected 'who sees what' system (information.py InformationItem/Exposure with its own credibility and per-actor visibility) that any real observation layer must reconcile with.
capabilities.required_replacement: (1) Per-world (not process-global) observation-model binding, attached by the compiler from the plan with wildcard/path-pattern support; (2) model families for counts, categorical, ordinal, and censored outcomes plus a fitting path (fit sd/bias/house-effects from historical measurement-vs-truth pairs, recording calibration_status honestly); (3) enforce reported_at/as-of at the assimilation boundary and visibility at the actor boundary; (4) unify Observation with information.py's ledger so actor-visible observations become Exposures that agent policies actually condition on; (5) make rollout emit observations from latent state so forecasts are over observables, not latents.
capabilities.empirical_test_needed: Calibration-against-reality test: take a real measurement/truth paired dataset (e.g. pre-election polls vs certified results, or Enron 'tracking shows reply' vs actual reply timestamps), fit the observation model, and verify held-out likelihood/coverage (PIT histogram approximately uniform, empirical sd within CI of fitted sd). Integration test: a full rollout must generate observations at the right delays, actors must only condition on visibility-permitted ones, and assimilating an observation before its reported_at must be rejected (today probe P2 shows it is silently accepted).

capabilities.capability: 24. Posterior assimilation (particle weighting / P(World | evidence) updates)
capabilities.current_behavior: ParticlePosterior (posterior.py:40-155) implements normalized weights, likelihood reweighting via observation_model_for, ESS (line 63), threshold-triggered systematic resampling with ancestry (lines 88-114), provenance-respecting rejuvenation jitter (lines 116-131), expectation/distribution readouts, and a posterior predictive check. It runs and passes two synthetic tests (tests/test_world_model_v2.py:416-459). It is completely DISCONNECTED from the forecasting system: WorldModelV2Run.run (rollout.py:79-90) samples particles, rolls each branch independently, and projects — no observation is ever assimilated; WorldBranch.weight (state.py:225) stays 1.0 forever (grep confirms no mutation anywhere); contract.project (contracts.py:44) consumes those weights but they are always uniform; no experiment or reference world imports ParticlePosterior. Empirically confirmed defects: (P1) posterior_predictive_check crashes with TypeError when any particle lacks the latent path (posterior.py:152 passes None into GaussianMeasurement.likelihood_pure's float()); (P4) _rejuvenate clamps sampled floats without a dist to the hardcoded [0,1] default (posterior.py:129-131) — a sampled value of 5000.0 (dollars, days) becomes 1.0 after one resample, silently destroying non-unit-scale latents; (P8) particles whose latent is missing get flat likelihood 0.5 (posterior.py:76) which is compared against Gaussian DENSITIES (up to ~8.0 at sd=0.05) — a probability-mass-vs-density category error that arbitrarily reweights sparse worlds; (P2) no reported_at/as-of guard — future observations assimilate freely (information leak); (P3) contradictory evidence is handled only by the 1e-12 likelihood floor (posterior.py:77), which also makes the normalize() 'weight_collapse' branch (lines 54-58) unreachable via assimilate — irreconcilable observations silently produce near-uniform weights with no conflict signal; distribution() (lines 140-145) stringifies values so a continuous posterior yields one bucket per particle (P6: 8 particles → 8 'buckets'); rejuvenation jitters each field independently (fixed sd 0.05), destroying the correlations InitialStateModel established; discrete/categorical sampled latents are never rejuvenated (only isinstance(value,float)), so categorical diversity collapses permanently after resampling; there is no batch/joint assimilation, no interleaving of assimilation with rollout time-stepping, and no test that assimilating observations recovers a known latent against ground truth (only 'moved past 0.6' on a hand-built 2-mode ensemble).
capabilities.why_insufficient: For a production world model, assimilation is the mechanism that keeps forecasts conditioned on incoming evidence (poll streams, engagement telemetry, reply events). Here it is a 155-line demonstration that no forecast path calls, with category errors in the likelihood treatment of missing latents, a rejuvenation step that corrupts any latent outside [0,1] and breaks joint structure, no temporal semantics (as-of/reported_at ignored), no contradictory-evidence or source-conflict handling beyond a numeric floor, unusable continuous readouts, and no interaction with the rollout engine — you cannot assimilate at t and then continue simulating, which is the entire point of a filter.
capabilities.required_replacement: (1) Wire assimilation into WorldModelV2Run as a filter loop: roll all branches to each observation's reported_at, reweight WorldBranch.weight through the observation model, resample/rejuvenate, continue — with as-of enforcement rejecting not-yet-visible observations; (2) fix the likelihood layer: consistent densities vs masses, explicit missing-latent policy (marginalize under the particle's own prior, not constant 0.5), likelihood_pure on every model, None-latent guards in PPC; (3) scale-aware, correlation-preserving rejuvenation (jitter proportional to each latent's declared sd, or MCMC moves targeting the joint posterior) and categorical rejuvenation; (4) conflict handling: per-source reliability as an inferred latent so contradictory observations shift mass toward 'source X is wrong' instead of flattening weights; (5) weighted-quantile/KDE continuous readouts; (6) per-run (non-global) observation-model resolution.
capabilities.empirical_test_needed: Sequential-recovery and calibration test: simulate ground-truth trajectories, emit timed observation streams, run the interleaved rollout+assimilation filter, and verify (a) posterior log-score/Brier on the terminal outcome improves monotonically with observation count vs the prior-only run, (b) credible-interval coverage is nominal, (c) ESS stays healthy over 1000+ assimilations without categorical-diversity collapse, (d) latents on non-[0,1] scales survive resampling unchanged in distribution (today they collapse to 1.0), (e) a stream containing planted contradictory/misleading sources yields an explicit conflict signal and correct truth recovery. Then real data: assimilate Enron thread-activity prefixes mid-horizon and show improved reply forecasts vs the current no-assimilation v2_predict arm.

capabilities.current_behavior: There are FOUR disjoint retrieval stacks; only one works well, and NONE is wired into the V2 world model. (1) swm/engine/retrieval.py is the real one: google_news() (Google News RSS, dated Passages), wikipedia(), bing_snippets(), plus keyed Serper/Brave/Tavily overlays auto-detected from env (retrieval.py:224-269); multi_search() fans out in a ThreadPoolExecutor and dedups on text[:80].lower(). I probed it live: asof_google_news returned 5 in-window dated headlines for as-of 2026-06-01, 2023-03-01, and 2021-06-01, and asof_wikipedia returned the real revision-as-of article. SceneGrounder (grounding.py:170) is a genuine 2-round pipeline: LLM plans a checklist+queries, retrieval fetches, LLM distills facts-with-citations, a 'who is favored' deepening round runs if no directional standing, and it abstains below min_coverage=0.4 or under 3 passages. This is consumed only by the V1 engine (front_door.py:117) and eval harnesses. (2) swm/api/live_search.py uses DuckDuckGo Lite, which the newer module's own header documents as returning 202 anti-bot challenges ('why the old live_search.py starved every question'), i.e. mostly dead. (3) swm/retrieval/asof_news.py + gdelt.py hit the GDELT DOC API: probed live it returned 0 headlines; the recorded exp093 run got news for only 6 of 150 questions (experiments/results/exp093_news_predictions.json), so the 'information parity' arm was actually the no-news arm on 96% of items. LiveNewsAdapter in news_context.py:65 is an explicit IMPLEMENTED=False stub. (4) swm/api/retrieval.py Retriever/asof_retriever is a thin dict-server. Critically, the V2 package (swm/world_model_v2/) imports NO retrieval module: compile_world(question, llm, evidence: str, ...) takes a caller-supplied free-form string, truncated to 4000 chars into the LLM prompt (compiler.py:103-112), and facade.forecast(architecture='world_model_v2', evidence='') defaults it to empty (facade.py:75-86). The flagship WMv2 benchmark runner admits it in writing: 'corpus carries no as-of evidence snapshots; F3/F4 are question-text-only' and the V2 arm is crowd-price + hazard rescaling with zero retrieved evidence (wmv2_forecastbench_run.py:171-176).
capabilities.why_insufficient: For a production general social world model, evidence retrieval must be an internal, automatic stage that populates the world state; here it is (a) not connected to WMv2 at all — the compiler receives whatever string the caller pastes in, with no schema, no per-fact timestamps, no provenance survival past a 12-hex-char sha1 prompt hash; (b) headline/snippet-only — no full-article fetch, no document store, no embedding/semantic ranking (similarity everywhere is token-overlap Jaccard: corpus.py:41, context.py:56, social_context.py:28); (c) fragile monoculture — the only working keyless source is Google News RSS regex-scraping plus Wikipedia; DDG and GDELT are dead in this environment, Bing is best-effort HTML scraping, so a Google format change kills grounding silently (failures degrade to [] by design); (d) unvalidated at production scale — the SceneGrounder pipeline's measured runs are n=44 deliberation questions (exp097/exp098) and a 127-question crowd grade where the engine scored skill_vs_crowd = -1.52 (exp093_large_crowd_grade.json), i.e. the retrieval-grounded engine is currently far WORSE than the crowd; (e) evidence quality is never scored against ground truth relevance — coverage is a substring heuristic (grounding.py:224-229 matches checklist[:24] lowercase into fact text).
capabilities.required_replacement: A first-class evidence layer inside WMv2: an EvidenceBundle typed object (per-item text, source URL, publish timestamp, retrieval timestamp, content hash) produced by a multi-provider retriever (keyed search API + news archive + Wikipedia revisions + full-text fetch), semantic ranking (embeddings, not token Jaccard), automatic invocation from facade.forecast/compile_world so the compiler and init_state consume typed evidence items instead of a 4000-char string, and per-fact provenance carried into WorldExecutionPlan latents/quantities. Provider health checks must be loud (GDELT/DDG should be removed or fixed, not silently return []).
capabilities.empirical_test_needed: (1) Wiring test: call facade.forecast(architecture='world_model_v2', ...) on 20 live questions without passing evidence and trace (mock urllib) that zero retrieval HTTP calls occur — proving evidence is caller-supplied only. (2) Provider audit: run asof_google_news/gdelt/ddg/bing on 100 diverse questions and report per-provider non-empty rates (expect GDELT~0, DDG~0). (3) Relevance eval: human-label 200 retrieved passages for decision-relevance to their questions; measure precision@k of the token-overlap ranking vs an embedding ranker. (4) End-to-end value: re-run the 127-question grade_vs_crowd with retrieval ablated on/off and report whether retrieval moves skill_vs_crowd from -1.52 toward 0 at all.

capabilities.capability: 8. As-of evidence verification (leakage defenses, timestamps, snapshots, hashes)
capabilities.current_behavior: Multiple real but disconnected gates exist. STRONG pieces: AsOfStore.query hard-filters timestamp>as_of and rejects untimestamped inserts and as_of-less queries (asof_store.py:50-82); TimestampedCorpus.as_of uses strict < with a defense-in-depth _assert_no_future re-check that raises LeakageError (corpus.py:45-86); ingestion store.history_asof is a strict-< SQL gate (store.py:107); asof_google_news parses every RSS pubDate and drops items after cutoff even if Google's before:/after: operators leak (retrieval.py:105-117, unit-tested with a smuggled 'WINNER DECLARED' item in test_agent_engine.py:520); asof_wikipedia fetches the actual article REVISION as of the cutoff via rvstart/rvdir=older (retrieval.py:121-163) — I verified both work live for 2026, 2023, and 2021 as-of dates. score_grounding computes an asof_valid axis flagging post-cutoff fact dates (contract.py:144-148). WEAK pieces: (a) the two live as-of guards both allow a +86400s slack — retrieval.py:110 'ts > as_of_ts + 86400' and asof_market.py:35 'r[0] <= ts + 86400' — so headlines and price candles up to a full day AFTER the as-of pass the gate; against the actual corpus (661 Manifold markets, median as_of→resolve span 5.2 days, minimum 6 MINUTES) a resolution-announcing headline can legally leak in; (b) swm/eval/leakage.py full_gate ('runs as a CI gate') is imported by NOTHING except tests/test_general_swm.py, where it is fed hand-built toy dicts — no production pipeline (grade_vs_crowd, tiered_ablation, flywheel, wmv2 runners) ever calls it; (c) the content-hash 'contamination probe' is exact sha256 of the full lowercased text (leakage.py:43-53) — defeated by any single-character paraphrase, admitted 'known-insufficient' in its own docstring; (d) asof_retriever (api/retrieval.py:61) enforces nothing: 'Leakage discipline is the caller's'; (e) assert_asof (event_backtest.py:33) is a string comparison of two metadata date strings, not an evidence check; (f) model-memorization control is a single hardcoded CUTOFF_TS=1719792000 (2024-07-01) in forecasting_corpus.py:21, while exp008's docstring claims a 'Jan-2026 model cutoff' for the same LLM family, and exp097/098 grade on ForecastBench rounds dated 2025-06-08..2025-10-26 asserted 'post-cutoff' with no verification — those resolutions may be inside deepseek-chat's training data; (g) there are NO evidence snapshots: retrieved passages are never persisted or hashed (exp093's plain-JSON news cache is absent from disk; SceneDossiers are not archived; compiler provenance keeps only a truncated sha1 of the whole prompt), so no backtest is reproducible or auditable after the fact; (h) SceneGrounder defaults to LIVE multi_search when the caller forgets search_fn — facade's baseline:grounded_direct path (facade.py:100) with a historical as_of and no evidence string will happily ground on TODAY'S news; (i) tests are green (11 passed for the three named test files) but test_retrieval_live.py touches no network and test_leakage_gate.py tests a 4-line list comprehension on 4 synthetic events.
capabilities.why_insufficient: The gates that are physically sound (AsOfStore, TimestampedCorpus) guard toy in-memory stores used only by tests and one 2024-era experiment (exp008, which reads committed data/mf_*.json files); the pipelines that actually run backtests rely on per-call convention: the right search_fn factory must be passed at every call site, nothing audits the evidence that actually reached the LLM, and the one 'full gate' is dead code in production paths. The one-day slack windows are concrete leak vectors against a corpus whose median question lives 5 days. There is no per-model training-cutoff registry, so 'cutoff_clean' and 'post-cutoff' are unverifiable assertions that contradict each other across files. Zero snapshotting means a leak can never be proven or disproven retroactively, and reruns silently retrieve different evidence (Google News results change), making every reported number unreproducible. Provider-reported pubDates are trusted blindly — a post-resolution retitled article keeps its original pubDate and passes the gate.
capabilities.required_replacement: One mandatory evidence gateway on the forecast path: every passage that reaches any LLM prompt must flow through a gate object that (1) requires a parseable publish timestamp strictly <= as_of with zero slack (make the slack a parameter defaulting to 0, alert when nonzero); (2) persists the full evidence bundle to an append-only store keyed by (question_id, as_of) with per-item sha256 and a bundle hash recorded in the run's provenance; (3) refuses to run a historical as_of with a live search_fn (fail loud, not silent live retrieval); (4) carries a per-model training-cutoff registry and marks each item contaminated/clean against the ACTUAL model id used; (5) adds a time-travel probe (redact outcome, ask the model to recover it from the retrieved context) and near-duplicate detection (shingled n-gram / MinHash) instead of exact-hash equality; (6) wires full_gate into grade_vs_crowd/tiered_ablation/flywheel so a violation fails the run, not just a unit test.
capabilities.empirical_test_needed: (1) Slack exploit: select corpus items with resolve_ts - as_of < 86400 (they exist; min span is 6 minutes), run asof_google_news at as_of, and count retrieved headlines whose pubDate falls in (as_of, resolve_ts+X] that state the outcome — quantifies the live leak. (2) Memorization probe: ask deepseek-chat (no evidence) the outcomes of the exp098 ForecastBench 2025 rounds; accuracy significantly above class base rate falsifies the 'post-cutoff, leak-free' claim behind exp097/098 headline results. (3) Reproducibility: run the same 40-question grade_vs_crowd twice a week apart and diff retrieved passages per question — nonzero diff with no stored snapshot proves results are unauditable. (4) Gate-coverage trace: instrument every llm(prompt) call in a full backtest and verify what fraction of prompts containing retrieved text passed through ANY leakage check (expect ~0% via full_gate today). (5) Retitle attack: inject a fixture passage with pre-as_of pubDate but outcome-revealing text and confirm nothing catches it (only exact-hash dedup exists).

No code anywhere auto-populates it for V2 — the wmv2_* runners build worlds from dataset files and the forecastbench runner uses crowd price + hazard only. If you build the Phase-2 evidence layer, the integration point is this `evidence` parameter plus init_state.py (which classifies fields OBSERVED/LLM/PRIOR from the same string). (b) Inclusive vs strict as-of conventions differ deliberately: asof_store <=, corpus.py and context.py strict <, entity_context strict < with an inline comment why; don't 'unify' blindly. (f) In experiments/wmv2_forecastbench_run.py:119, elapsed_frac = as_of/resolve_ts on ABSOLUTE unix timestamps ≈ 0.9999 clamped to 0.95 for every item — the V2 'temporal hazard rescaling' arm degenerates to a constant exponent g^0.95 on the Platt-calibrated crowd; the fitted-vs-ablation comparisons in that file are testing a constant. (g) GDELT (asof_news.py, gdelt.py, gdelt_index.py) is unreachable/rate-limited from this environment (live probe: 0 results; exp093: 6/150 hits) — any plan assuming GDELT as the as-of news source is dead on arrival; Google News RSS is the only working keyless as-of source and it works even for 2021 as-of dates (verified).
