(i) Registry store sha256 hashes (world_model_v2/registry/store.py) are for mechanism-record integrity, not evidence — do not mistake them for evidence snapshotting; no evidence bundle is hashed or archived anywhere. (j) The engine's recorded honest performance with full as-of grounding is skill_vs_crowd = -1.52 on n=124 (exp093_large_crowd_grade.json) — retrieval plumbing correctness is not the current accuracy bottleneck claim to beat; the crowd is.

capabilities.capability: 13. Parameter estimation (how mechanism/policy parameters are fitted)
capabilities.current_behavior: There is no shared estimation subsystem; every reference world hand-writes its own fitter, all train-only and genuinely executed on real data: enron.py fit_mechanisms():123 (empirical hazard over 5 buckets, Laplace-smoothed recipient/pair rates, tercile workload multipliers, hour/weekday multipliers, check_rate = 1/median-delay), fit_hetero_sd():351 (log-rate dispersion, clamped [0.1,0.6], with an unexplained /2 'rate noise' correction at line 364); actor_cognition.py fit_action_policy():158 (batch-GD logistic over 18 LLM features + anchor logit, L2=0.03, lr=0.15, iters=600 fixed, (w_anchor-1)^2 shrinkage; fitted weights persisted in wmv2_enron_actor_ladder.json _meta.policy, n_train=300); behavior_games.py fit_dist():60 (KDE, Silverman bandwidth), fit_priors():92 (triple nested 11-point grid over level-k simplex + 11-point slope grid, each candidate scored by a quantile-matched W1 against 300 simulated draws), fit_beta():148 (7-point grid over trembling sd); omnibehavior.py fit_stats():41 (shrunk per-user rates + momentum lift); higgs.py fit_logistic():84 and fit_q():113 (bisection moment-match); wmv2_enron_maxcap.py _platt():63 (1-D Platt recalibration on a train tail slice). The one general estimator — policy.py fit_mixture_weights():158 (exponentiated-gradient over Fehr-Schmidt mixture weights, finite-difference W1 gradients) and choice.py fit_game_policy():268 (hierarchical: per-game lambda/sd/tau/slope profiling + jointly-pooled FS mixture, leave-one-game-out transfer) — is UNTRACKED in git, has ZERO tests importing it, and its runner experiments/wmv2_behaviorbench_policy_run.py has never produced a results file (experiments/results/wmv2_behaviorbench_policy.json does not exist).
capabilities.why_insufficient: Every executed fitter is welded to one dataset's feature layout and outcome family (email reply hazard, 7 named games, TikTok passive events, one Twitter graph); nothing generalizes: a new domain requires writing a new estimator by hand. All fits are point estimates — no standard errors, no posterior over parameters (the only parameter uncertainty anywhere is a HARDCODED lognormal sd 0.4 around q in higgs.py:211). Grid searches minimize a noisy 300-simulation W1 at a fixed seed (fit_beta, fit_priors), so the selected value is partly Monte-Carlo luck. fit_action_policy has fixed hyperparameters, no convergence check, no CV. posterior.py's ParticlePosterior (assimilate/reweight/resample) is never used to estimate any mechanism parameter from data in any benchmark — it exists only in unit tests. uncertainty.estimate_parameter is a provenance gate (reject/broaden), not an estimator. And where the fitting was validated, the fitted structural models mostly LOST to their own statistical baselines: A5 aggregate W1n 0.0575 vs train-histogram A1 0.0376 (wmv2_behaviorbench.json); higgs M_sel vs fitted logistic H1 CI [-0.000525, 0.000145] — a tie at best.
capabilities.required_replacement: A single estimation layer that any mechanism registers into: declared parameters with priors, a likelihood or simulator-based (W1/ABC) objective, shared optimizers with convergence diagnostics, parameter uncertainty (bootstrap or Laplace/posterior) propagated into rollout particles, hierarchical partial pooling as a first-class facility (finish and validate choice.py's design), and automatic train/val protocol with recovery tests. The mechanism registry's parameter_source strings should point at fitted records, not prose.
capabilities.empirical_test_needed: (a) Parameter-recovery suite: simulate data from known parameters through each fitter and verify recovery with quantified error and calibrated uncertainty intervals. (b) Actually execute experiments/wmv2_behaviorbench_policy_run.py and check P_fit vs A1 (in-distribution) and P_logo vs T_pool (transfer) — the transfer regime is the only one where the hierarchical model can justify itself, since A1/A2 already win in-distribution. (c) Seed-sensitivity test on fit_beta/fit_priors: refit at 10 seeds and measure selected-parameter variance.

capabilities.current_behavior: actor_cognition.py implements the full pipeline and it genuinely runs end-to-end on real data with a real LLM (DeepSeek V3; 660 metered calls in the committed wmv2_enron_actor_ladder.json): interpret():108 makes ONE LLM call producing a typed 18-dim Interpretation (11 clamped [0,1] dims + 7 intent one-hots, fixed order at lines 33-39), abstaining (None) on parse failure; ActionPolicy.p_engage():153 maps features + metadata-anchor logit to engagement probability via the train-fitted logistic; action_distribution():195 splits that mass into a 5-action pack with HARDCODED shape constants (clarify <=0.35*p_engage*needs_clarification, now_frac = 0.35+0.5*urgency, delegate <=0.5*passive*needs_delegation); hidden_state_latents():220 declares 3 per-particle latents (responsiveness with fitted dispersion, attention, obligation_sensitivity, one +0.3 correlation); attention_transition():257 is a mean-reverting process with hardcoded tau=0.5d, noise 0.08, daytime bonus +/-0.10; relationship_strength():270 uses hardcoded saturation constant 8.0 and /5.0 reciprocity scaling. enron.py v2_predict_actor():370 composes all of this into the C2-C6 ladder through the real event runtime. 40 offline tests pass (2.2s) but every LLM in tests is a canned-JSON stub.
capabilities.why_insufficient: Three independent failures. (1) VALIDATION IS NULL: in the committed benchmark (wmv2_enron_actor_ladder.json), no structured level beats the fitted-metadata bar E1 — C6_vs_E1 Δ+0.00627 Brier CI [-0.00569, +0.01869] time-forward, and every single C(l)_vs_C(l-1) ladder-step CI spans zero on both splits; at n=120/60 with base rates 0.092/0.017 the study is far too underpowered to detect anything. C6 even trails the demoted scalar arm C0 (Δ+0.00219). (2) UNIVERSALITY IS COSMETIC: the 'negotiation/election/platform/institution' packs at ACTION_PACKS:182 are only tuples of names; action_distribution treats slot0/slot1 as act-now/act-later, so in the election pack URGENCY controls the act_support vs act_oppose split and in negotiation NEEDS_DELEGATION maps to 'delay' — categorical semantic nonsense that the universality test (test_actor_cognition.py:157) never catches because it only checks normalization. Only messaging (Enron) and platform (OmniBehavior) ever touched real data. (3) HARDCODED PROCESS CONSTANTS: every temporal-process parameter is an invented prior, and in v2_predict_actor the time-of-day process is fed the constant SEND hour (enron.py:455, hour=ex.feats['hour']) for the whole 14-day rollout — the diurnal cycle never actually advances with the simulation clock, so the 'dynamic attention' level C4 partially tests a stuck clock.
capabilities.required_replacement: A per-domain-fitted interpretation schema (validate which of the 18 dims carry signal; drop or refit the rest), semantically grounded action packs whose split structure is fitted per domain rather than borrowed from messaging slot positions, process parameters (tau, noise, diurnal amplitude, relationship constants) estimated from data with the clock actually driving hour-of-day, and a powered validation before any of it is called production.
capabilities.empirical_test_needed: (a) Powered re-run of the C-ladder with n>=1000 LLM-scored held-out rows (power analysis first: at base rate 0.09 and observed deltas ~0.004 Brier, n=120 has near-zero power). (b) Per-dimension ablation of the 18 features on train to identify which earn nonzero weight out-of-sample. (c) A genuine second-domain test: fit the SAME schema on real negotiation or committee-vote data and show transfer; the current negotiation 'test' with stub JSON proves only that arithmetic normalizes. (d) Fix the stuck hour and re-measure C4_vs_C3.

capabilities.capability: 15. Typed action policies (does an LLM mint probabilities? is anything learned/calibrated?)
capabilities.current_behavior: Three coexisting policy paths with contradictory contracts. PATH A (reference worlds, the advertised one): LLM emits semantic features only; probability comes from the train-fitted anchored logistic (actor_cognition.py ActionPolicy:141, fit_action_policy:158) — genuinely learned, executed, with ECE reported and the with-zero-weights-reduces-to-anchor property tested. PATH B (the domain-general runtime): transitions.py AgentDecisionOperator._policy():167-188 sends _DECIDE_PROMPT and the LLM DIRECTLY RETURNS the action distribution ('Return ONLY JSON: {"p": {"<action>": <0..1>}}' at line 152), which is normalized and sampled — the LLM mints the probabilities outright, contradicting the module docstring ('it cannot ... emit coefficients'). This is the operator materialize.py operators_from_plan():96 wires for EVERY compiled world, and it has NEVER run with a real LLM anywhere — all tests and the compiler acceptance test use llm=None, which degrades to a UNIFORM policy (line 169). PATH C (demoted but still live): enron.py content_multiplier():181 has the LLM mint a scalar reply_propensity that multiplies the fitted rate (bounded 0.45+1.75*prop), executed in wmv2_enron_maxcap (1460 calls). Additionally BeliefUpdateOperator.apply():247-263 moves beliefs with invented constants (0.9 step scale, 0.95 target, ad-hoc 'compat' damping, x0.5) yet is registered validated=True; and swm/experimental/behavior_models.py has the LLM emit both a chosen action AND a self-assessed probability p (_DECIDE_PROMPT:71) — quarantined, disabled-by-default, behavior-trained backends are pure stubs that raise BackendUnavailable.
capabilities.why_insufficient: The calibrated no-LLM-minting contract exists ONLY inside hand-built reference worlds; the path a production user would actually hit (compiler -> materialize -> AgentDecisionOperator) is either a uniform coin-flip (llm=None) or raw LLM-minted probabilities with zero learning, zero calibration, zero validation — the exact failure mode the repo's own Enron round documented (E3 raw LLM: ECE 0.16-0.33, Brier 0.151/0.261 vs base-rate 0.087/0.017; E3_vs_E2 Δ+0.078 and +0.253, i.e., the raw LLM is far worse than hashed bag-of-words). Even in PATH A, only p_engage is learned; the typed SPLIT of that mass (which action) uses hardcoded messaging-shaped constants never fitted in any domain ('prior_backed pending per-domain fits' at actor_cognition.py:199 — the pending fit does not exist). ActionPolicy.p_engage zips w with features and SILENTLY TRUNCATES on length mismatch (line 154) — a policy fitted on 22 dims scored with 18-dim features returns a wrong number with no error. Where PATH A was validated (capability 14), the lift was null; and the E4 'ensemble' arm that was supposed to price call-matched LLM sampling is broken by memoization (see load-bearing notes), so the strongest direct-LLM comparator is understated.
capabilities.required_replacement: Delete or gate PATH B's probability-minting: AgentDecisionOperator should call the same fitted-policy machinery (interpretation features -> fitted calibration layer) with the LLM restricted to feature extraction, or else be registered experimental/uncalibrated and refused by default. Fit the action-split constants per domain. Add a feature-schema check (dimension + ordering hash) to ActionPolicy. Wire Platt/conformal recalibration into the V2 policy outputs (currently only bolted onto E1/E5 inside one experiment script).
capabilities.empirical_test_needed: (a) Run AgentDecisionOperator with a real LLM on any held-out benchmark and measure ECE/Brier of its minted p_dist against the fitted-policy path on identical rows — this number does not exist anywhere in the repo. (b) Fit the action_distribution split fractions on Enron train (reply_now vs reply_later vs clarify are observable from reply delays/threading) and test whether the hardcoded 0.35/0.5 priors fall inside the fitted CIs. (c) Unit test that ActionPolicy raises on feature-length mismatch. (d) Fit BeliefUpdateOperator's step constants on any belief-change dataset or downgrade its registration from validated.

capabilities.current_behavior: The runtime can host multi-actor shared state — per-actor information sets (information ledger + observable_view at transitions.py:114), institution validation, and InstitutionalVoteOperator reading every participant's current_action — but that coupling is exercised only in offline unit tests with stub/uniform policies (test_world_model_v2.py:78 alice's decision read by bob's vote; :323 three nurses). In the benchmarks, 'interaction' is: (1) BehaviorBench — belief simulation INSIDE one actor's closed-form policy: particle_action():167 draws 40 samples of the cross-game-fitted partner latent internally (lines 193, 205) to compute an expected-utility best response; the runtime-path v2_game_world():243 adds a partner Entity whose PartnerResponse operator fires AFTER the actor has chosen and writes partner.current_action which NOTHING reads — the readout is quantities['choice'] only (line 338), so the world-level partner is decoration verifying trace consistency (w1 0.016-0.11 vs the fast path), not causal coupling. (2) Higgs — genuine multi-actor state coupling (a user's activation increments sampled followers' exposure mid-window, higgs.py v2_contagion_predict():195-238) but implemented as bespoke arrays entirely OUTSIDE the V2 runtime: no WorldState, no events, no deltas. Structurally, operators receive only (world, event, rng) — RolloutEngine.run_branch (rollout.py:29) never exposes the queue — so no actor's action can schedule an event or exposure for another actor; the 'endogenous' scheduling type is registered in events.py:26 but nothing ever produces an endogenous event.
capabilities.why_insufficient: For a production social world model, interaction means actor A's action changes what actor B observes before B decides. No benchmarked code path does this through the runtime: Enron worlds contain exactly one entity (the sender is a string), OmniBehavior is single-user, BehaviorBench interaction is a one-shot internal expectation (defensible as beliefs, but it never touches the shared world), and Higgs bypasses the runtime entirely. The event system cannot express action->event chains (no operator can schedule), so message threads, offers/counteroffers, cascades, or retaliation are inexpressible inside the 'universal' engine — the DeferredCompletion hack in enron.py:558 (a follow-up action forced to ride pre-existing hazard events, with its delay re-derived from a hash-seeded RNG each check) exists precisely because scheduling is impossible. Empirically, where interaction mechanisms were scored: the partner-belief ablation shows real signal (A5 0.0575 vs no_interaction 0.1088 aggregate W1n; 0.0708 vs 0.1605 on interaction games) yet A5 still LOSES to the plain train histogram A1 (0.0376/0.0534), and both Higgs rounds concluded within-window contagion adds nothing over the static closed form (Msel_vs_no_rollout Δ-6.3e-05 CI [-0.000156, +0.000031]) and ties the as-of logistic. So shared-world interaction has never beaten a non-interactive baseline on held-out data anywhere in the repo.
capabilities.required_replacement: First, give operators the ability to emit follow-up events (return proposed events alongside the StateDelta, validated against the event-type registry and queued by RolloutEngine) so action->exposure->decision chains exist. Second, make PartnerResponse (or its successor) causally load-bearing: the actor's payoff/next decision must read the realized partner action, and the readout must depend on the joint terminal state. Third, port Higgs-style contagion into the runtime (exposure as information items, activation as decisions) or explicitly declare the runtime unsuitable for population-scale diffusion and keep the vectorized path as a registered mechanism family. Fourth, find a dataset where sequential interaction is measurable (multi-round negotiation, email thread back-and-forth, alternating CMV exchanges) — one-shot games structurally cannot exercise this, as the code itself logs.
capabilities.empirical_test_needed: A held-out benchmark where the target depends on a realized multi-actor chain: e.g., Enron thread continuation (predict sender's follow-up given recipient's simulated reply/non-reply — requires two coupled actors in one world), scored against a no-coupling ablation with paired CIs; plus a runtime-vs-bespoke consistency check for a ported Higgs cascade (the runtime path must reproduce v2_contagion_predict within Monte-Carlo error at equal particle count, and its wall-clock cost must be measured — 4000 users x 12 steps x 30 particles through Python operator dispatch may be prohibitive).

result.load_bearing_notes: CALL PATHS: experiments/wmv2_*.py import swm.api.deepseek_backend.default_chat_fn — returns None without DEEPSEEK_API_KEY (then hf_chat_fn if HF_TOKEN). wmv2_enron_maxcap silently runs with LLM arms OFF when key is missing (chat=None branch); wmv2_enron_actor_run and wmv2_behaviorbench_run SystemExit instead. JSON parsing everywhere is swm.engine.grounding.parse_json (LLM parse failure -> abstention/None paths, never exceptions). Reference worlds import the runtime lazily INSIDE functions (enron.py:209-214, behavior_games.py:248-253), so module import never touches the runtime. FEATURE CONTRACT: Interpretation.features() = 11 floats in FEATURE_DIMS order then 7 intent one-hots in INTENTS order = 18 dims (actor_cognition.py:36-39,61-64). ActionPolicy.p_engage zips w with feats and SILENTLY TRUNCATES on length mismatch (actor_cognition.py:154) — OmniBehavior policies are 22-dim (4 metadata + 18 interp, wmv2_omnibehavior_run.py:150-156); scoring a policy on the wrong layout produces a wrong number with no error. REPRODUCIBILITY GOTCHA: per-branch RNGs are seeded with hash(world.branch_id) & 0xFFFF (enron.py:280, 539, 566; behavior_games has no such issue) — str hash is salted per process, so hazard event times and deferred-reply delays are NOT reproducible across Python processes unless PYTHONHASHSEED is pinned. BENCHMARK BUG: in wmv2_enron_actor_run, metered() memoizes on (id(fn), prompt) and the E4 'ensemble' jitters only subject[:200-k] for k in (0,7,13) — for subjects <187 chars all three prompts collapse to E3's memoized prompt, so E4==E3 exactly (confirmed: 8/20 forensic rows byte-identical, aggregate metrics identical to 4 decimals on both splits); the direct-ensemble comparator is fake in that run. RESULTS PROVENANCE: committed results exist for wmv2_enron_maxcap/forensic, wmv2_enron_actor_ladder/forensic, wmv2_behaviorbench, wmv2_omnibehavior_v2, wmv2_higgs, wmv2_higgs_nonlinear (untracked, written this session); NO results exist for wmv2_behaviorbench_policy_run.py — policy.py, registry/ (incl. families/choice.py), and both new runners are UNTRACKED in git (git status), i.e., Phase-4/6/7 work-in-progress on branch claude/world-model-v2-production-64qwa6. HEADLINE NUMBERS an implementer should not overwrite: Enron E10 (scalar content) vs E1 fitted metadata: NO lift (Δ+0.00444 CI[-0.00486,+0.01649]); structured ladder C1-C6 vs E1: all null; raw LLM E3 catastrophically miscalibrated (ECE 0.33 person-disjoint); BehaviorBench A5 0.0575 loses to train histogram A1 0.0376 but interaction ablation is the repo's only significant mechanism positive; Higgs: contagion never beats as-of logistic. QUIRKS: hz_cum(p,j) in both Enron runners spreads a scalar over buckets via sum(fm.hazard[:j+2])/sum(fm.hazard) — j+2 is intentional (BUCKS starts at 1d but fm.BUCKETS starts at 4h). v2_predict_actor guarantees P(reply)=p_engage only via p_reply_target = p_eng/(1-p_delegate) capped at 0.97 (enron.py:398) — caps bind at high p. Terminal quantities in reference worlds are ad-hoc anonymous objects: world.quantities['choice'] = type('Q',(),{'value':...})() (behavior_games.py:291, enron.py:273,517) — not quantities.Quantity; anything expecting the Quantity API will break. register_event_type/_OPERATORS/_REGISTRY are process-global mutable dicts; reference worlds re-register on every call (idempotent overwrite). 'validated=True' in register_operator means 'the contract has unit tests', NOT 'parameters calibrated' — BeliefUpdateOperator's invented constants (transitions.py:254-256) ship as validated. The whole offline test suite (40 tests, 2.2s, all passing) uses canned-JSON stub LLMs; no test executes a real model. Platt calibration in wmv2_enron_maxcap fits on val = train[80%:] whose rows were ALSO used to fit fm (fit_mechanisms uses all of train) — mild circularity. In wmv2_behaviorbench_run, interpret() is called ONCE per game on the shared prompt with a generic actor string — population-level, not per-actor cognition; its dims shift KDE prior means by at most ±15% of range (behavior_games.py:162-164,177-187).
